title: Ginger Flaky German Bread Tagine
description: A yeasty, flaky German bread infused with the brightness of yuzu and the richness of peanut butter, perfect for a energizing breakfast or snack.

ingredients.name: Peanut butter
ingredients.notes: Smooth or crunchy, whichever you prefer.

instructions: In a small bowl, combine warm water and yeast. Let it sit for 5 minutes, until foamy.
In a large mixing bowl, whisk together whole wheat flour, salt, and turbinado sugar.
Add peanut butter, yuzu juice, and ginger to the flour mixture. Mix until well combined.
Add egg to the mixture and mix until a shaggy dough forms.
Add dry yeast mixture to the dough and mix until a sticky ball forms.
Knead the dough for 10 minutes, until smooth and elastic.
Place the dough in a lightly oiled bowl, cover it with plastic wrap, and let it rise in a warm place for 1 hour.
Punch down the dough and divide it into 8 equal pieces.
Roll each piece into a ball and then flatten it slightly into a disk shape.
Place the disks into a ceramic tagine or a large Dutch oven, leaving about 1 inch of space between each bread.
Dust the top of the bread with flaky sea salt and yuzu-cure.
Cover the tagine with a lid and bake for 30 minutes.
Remove the lid and continue baking for an additional 10-15 minutes, until the bread is golden brown.
Let the bread cool for 5 minutes before serving.
While the bread is baking, heat butter in a small saucepan over medium heat until melted.
Brush the melted butter over the warm bread before serving.
